source_paper_bibtex: @article{Mohammadi_2025, title={Parabolic Regularity of Spectral Functions}, volume={50}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2023.0010}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2023.0010}, number={3}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Mohammadi, Ashkan and Sarabi, Ebrahim}, year={2025}, month=aug, pages={2017–2046} }
context:
Section 5 (discussion immediately after Proposition 5.4), page 25.

To obtain a full characterization of the critical cone of spectral functions, we should know when the matrices \(\Lambda(Y )_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\) in Proposition 5.4 have a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition since the critical cone \(K_\theta(\lambda(X),\lambda(Y))\) can be often calculated rather easily. While this remains an open question for now and will be a subject of our future research, we show by an example below the possible role that the condition on \(\Lambda(Y )_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\) is playing in the calculation of the critical cone of spectral functions.
title: Characterize simultaneous ordered spectral decompositions needed for spectral-function critical cones
background_latex:
Let \(\mathbb{S}^n\) be the space of real \(n\times n\) symmetric matrices. For \(X\in\mathbb{S}^n\), let \(\lambda(X)\in\mathbb{R}^n\) denote the vector of eigenvalues sorted in nonincreasing order. Write an eigen-decomposition \(X=U\,\mathrm{Diag}(\lambda(X))U^\top\) with \(U\in O_n\). Let \(\mu_1>\cdots>\mu_r\) be the distinct eigenvalues of \(X\), and define index blocks
\[\alpha_m:=\{i\in\{1,\dots,n\}:\lambda_i(X)=\mu_m\},\qquad m=1,\dots,r,\]
so \(\{1,\dots,n\}=\bigcup_{m=1}^r \alpha_m\) is a partition.

A spectral function is a function \(g:\mathbb{S}^n\to\overline{\mathbb{R}}\) of the form \(g=\theta\circ\lambda\), where \(\theta:\mathbb{R}^n\to\overline{\mathbb{R}}\) is symmetric (permutation-invariant). Assume \(g\) is convex and lower semicontinuous, and let \(Y\in\partial g(X)\) be a (convex-analytic) subgradient. A known characterization (for convex spectral functions) implies there exists an orthogonal matrix \(U\in O_n(X)\cap O_n(Y)\) such that
\[X=U\,\mathrm{Diag}(\lambda(X))U^\top,\qquad Y=U\,\mathrm{Diag}(\lambda(Y))U^\top,\qquad \lambda(Y)\in\partial\theta(\lambda(X)).\]
For each block \(\alpha_m\), define the corresponding principal submatrices
\[\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}:=\big(\mathrm{Diag}(\lambda(Y))\big)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|},\qquad H_{m}:=U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|},\]
where \(H\in\mathbb{S}^n\) is a direction and \(U_{\alpha_m}\) denotes the submatrix of \(U\) with columns indexed by \(\alpha_m\).

Two symmetric matrices \(A,B\in\mathbb{S}^k\) are said to have a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition if there exists \(Q\in O_k\) such that \(Q^\top A Q\) and \(Q^\top B Q\) are both diagonal with diagonal entries sorted in nonincreasing order (equivalently, they are simultaneously diagonalizable in a way compatible with the eigenvalue orderings).
problem_definition_latex:
Give necessary and sufficient conditions (checkable from spectral data and/or algebraic relations) ensuring that, for a given convex spectral function \(g=\theta\circ\lambda\), a point \(X\in\mathbb{S}^n\), a subgradient \(Y\in\partial g(X)\), and a direction \(H\in\mathbb{S}^n\), the following property holds:

For every block \(m=1,\dots,r\) associated with the distinct eigenvalues of \(X\), the pair of matrices \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|}\) and \(H_m=U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|}\) admits a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition.

Equivalently, characterize when there exist orthogonal matrices \(Q_m\in O_{|\alpha_m|}\) such that both \(Q_m^\top \Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m} Q_m\) and \(Q_m^\top H_m Q_m\) are diagonal with their diagonal entries arranged in nonincreasing order.
latex:
Let \(\mathbb{S}^n\) be the space of real \(n\times n\) symmetric matrices. For \(X\in\mathbb{S}^n\), let \(\lambda(X)\in\mathbb{R}^n\) denote the vector of eigenvalues sorted in nonincreasing order. Write an eigen-decomposition \(X=U\,\mathrm{Diag}(\lambda(X))U^\top\) with \(U\in O_n\). Let \(\mu_1>\cdots>\mu_r\) be the distinct eigenvalues of \(X\), and define index blocks
\[\alpha_m:=\{i\in\{1,\dots,n\}:\lambda_i(X)=\mu_m\},\qquad m=1,\dots,r,\]
so \(\{1,\dots,n\}=\bigcup_{m=1}^r \alpha_m\) is a partition.

A spectral function is a function \(g:\mathbb{S}^n\to\overline{\mathbb{R}}\) of the form \(g=\theta\circ\lambda\), where \(\theta:\mathbb{R}^n\to\overline{\mathbb{R}}\) is symmetric (permutation-invariant). Assume \(g\) is convex and lower semicontinuous, and let \(Y\in\partial g(X)\) be a (convex-analytic) subgradient. A known characterization (for convex spectral functions) implies there exists an orthogonal matrix \(U\in O_n(X)\cap O_n(Y)\) such that

For each block \(\alpha_m\), define the corresponding principal submatrices
\[\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}:=\big(\mathrm{Diag}(\lambda(Y))\big)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|},\qquad H_{m}:=U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|},\]
where \(H\in\mathbb{S}^n\) is a direction and \(U_{\alpha_m}\) denotes the submatrix of \(U\) with columns indexed by \(\alpha_m\).

Give necessary and sufficient conditions (checkable from spectral data and/or algebraic relations) ensuring that, for a given convex spectral function \(g=\theta\circ\lambda\), a point \(X\in\mathbb{S}^n\), a subgradient \(Y\in\partial g(X)\), and a direction \(H\in\mathbb{S}^n\), the following property holds:

For every block \(m=1,\dots,r\) associated with the distinct eigenvalues of \(X\), the pair of matrices \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|}\) and \(H_m=U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\in\mathbb{S}^{|\alpha_m|}\) admits a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition.

open_problem_quote: To obtain a full characterization of the critical cone of spectral functions, we should know when the matrices \(\Lambda(Y )_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\) in Proposition 5.4 have a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition since the critical cone \(K_\theta(\lambda(X),\lambda(Y))\) can be often calculated rather easily. While this remains an open question for now and will be a subject of our future research, we show by an example below the possible role that the condition on \(\Lambda(Y )_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\) is playing in the calculation of the critical cone of spectral functions.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The property concerns existence of a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition, which can be tested numerically for random small matrices by checking commutativity/degeneracy structure and order compatibility, so automated searches for patterns or minimal obstructions are feasible. However, turning such experiments into a general characterization may require symbolic reasoning about eigenvalue multiplicities and ordering constraints beyond finite instance search.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: One can encode candidate algebraic characterizations (e.g., commutator conditions plus tie-handling rules) and score them on randomly generated instances where the decomposition existence can be decided computationally, providing an automatic fitness signal. The search space for correct necessary-and-sufficient conditions is large and the ordering aspect introduces case distinctions, so evolutionary search may help conjecture forms but is unlikely to fully solve the problem without substantial theoretical input.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper isolates a genuinely structural gap in second-order variational analysis of convex spectral functions \(g=\theta\circ\lambda\): while first-order subdifferential calculus is cleanly expressible via a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition of \(X\) and \(Y\in\partial g(X)\), the second-order theory (critical cones and second subderivatives) requires a finer, blockwise compatibility condition. Specifically, for each multiplicity block \(\alpha_m\) of \(X\), one needs that the diagonal block \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and the rotated directional block \(H_m=U_{\alpha_m}^\top H U_{\alpha_m}\) admit a simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition. Proposition 5.4 shows this condition is equivalent to equality cases in Fan-type trace inequalities on each block, but the paper explicitly notes that a practical necessary-and-sufficient characterization remains open.

Existing literature provides the surrounding machinery but not the missing characterization. Lewis (1999) supplies the foundational variational framework for spectral functions and the ordered simultaneous diagonalization appearing in subgradient characterizations. Torki (2001) provides the second-order eigenvalue expansions that the source paper uses to define \(\lambda''(X;H,W)\) and to build second-order tangent/epi-derivative chain rules. Rockafellar--Wets (1998) and subsequent work on parabolic regularity and twice epi-differentiability explain why an explicit description of the critical cone \(K_g(X,Y)\) is pivotal: it is the domain on which second subderivatives and second-order optimality conditions are evaluated. More recent matrix-optimization works (e.g., Cui--Ding 2019; Ding et al. 2018) highlight applications where such critical-cone descriptions are needed, but they typically impose additional structure (polyhedrality, nondegeneracy) rather than resolving the ordered simultaneous diagonalization question.

At present, the problem remains open: there is no general, checkable criterion stated in the available materials that characterizes when \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(H_m\) can be simultaneously diagonalized with eigenvalues aligned in nonincreasing order. Promising directions suggested by the surrounding theory include: (i) reducing the question to commutation and invariant-subspace conditions within each \(\alpha_m\)-block, then refining by handling ties in \(\Lambda(Y)_{\alpha_m\alpha_m}\) and \(H_m\) via majorization/rearrangement inequalities; and (ii) expressing the ordering-compatibility as an equality condition in a family of Fan/von Neumann trace inequalities restricted to multiplicity subspaces, potentially yielding algebraic conditions on spectral projectors and blockwise commutators.

sources.title: Nonsmooth analysis of eigenvalues
sources.theorem_or_result:
Develops variational-analytic tools for eigenvalue mappings and spectral functions, including subdifferential calculus that underpins the characterization of 
\(Y\in\partial(\theta\circ\lambda)(X)\) via simultaneous ordered spectral decompositions.
sources.connection: Provides the foundational first-order framework used in the source paper (e.g., Proposition 3.4) where the open problem arises when refining critical-cone characterizations beyond first order.

sources.title: Second-order directional derivatives of all eigenvalues of a symmetric matrix
sources.theorem_or_result: Establishes explicit second-order directional derivative formulas for ordered eigenvalues of symmetric matrices, including block-structure refinements when eigenvalues are multiple.
sources.connection: The source paper uses these expansions (Propositions 2.12.2) to define \(\lambda''(X;H,W)\) and to formulate second-order objects where the simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition condition appears blockwise.

sources.title: Variational Analysis
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces and systematizes tangent/second-order tangent constructions, epi-derivatives, second subderivatives, and parabolic regularity used throughout modern variational analysis.
sources.connection: Defines the second-order variational notions (parabolic regularity, second subderivative, critical cone) in which the open problem is posed; the simultaneous ordered spectral decomposition condition is needed to make critical-cone characterizations effective for spectral functions.

sources.title: Nonsmooth composite matrix optimization: strong Regularity, constraint nondegeneracy and beyond
sources.theorem_or_result: Studies second-order variational properties and regularity notions for composite matrix optimization involving spectral functions, including critical cones and second-order conditions under structural assumptions (e.g., polyhedrality).
sources.connection: Gives context where explicit critical-cone descriptions are needed; the open problem targets the missing algebraic/spectral characterization of the blockwise simultaneous ordered spectral decompositions appearing in such second-order analyses.

sources.title: Spectral operators of matrices
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops calculus for spectral operators (matrix functions defined via eigenvalues/singular values), including directional differentiability characterizations linked to eigen-structure and ordering.
sources.connection: Provides complementary operator-theoretic characterizations that may help translate the blockwise ordered simultaneous diagonalization requirement into checkable conditions on commutators and eigenvalue multiplicities.
